title: Autonomous Swarm Deployment: Pentagon Reevaluates Legacy Procurement

full_report.executive_summary: DoD CTO Emil Michael signals a major pivot away from legacy 'exquisite' platforms, emphasizing scalable, low-cost autonomous weapons.
full_report.technical_deep_dive: The integration of decentralized AI swarm logic across multi-domain unmanned systems enables robust operation in contested airspace without requiring continuous data-link connectivity.
full_report.strategic_impact: Traditional primes heavily invested in legacy platforms face significant downside risks, while emerging agile tech firms specializing in AI, autonomy, and mass-manufacturing of drones are positioned for major federal capital influx.
full_report.conclusion: The Pentagon's budgetary reallocation fundamentally alters the defense procurement landscape toward asymmetric warfare doctrines over the next decade.

title: Tiny drones making a buzz at the Berlin Air Show
description: The Breaking Defense team wraps up the last day of the Berlin Air Show.

title: Boeing bows out of Navy’s new trainer jet competition
description: Boeing said the T-7A Red Hawk training jet they are producing for the Air Force doesn’t fulfill Navy requirements.

title: Poland intends to buy two more squads of F-35s, minister says
description: Two extra squadrons of F-35s would double the existing order, from 32 to 64.

title: Better collaboration needed on SOCOM programs, says government watchdog
description: “We found that these [civilian] officials can’t effectively carry out the office’s oversight responsibilities as they can’t always get access to relevant program information or meetings,” GAO said while listing three recommendations.
